title: HopChain: Multi-Hop Data Synthesis for Generalizable Vision-Language Reasoning
task: Multi-hop vision-language reasoning for VLMs
key_results: Improved 20/24 benchmarks; 5.3-7.0 point drop without full multi-hop chains; 50+ point gains in ultra-long CoT
comments: Synthetic data generation for RLVR is practical but generalization claims rely on benchmark diversity rather than held-out task evaluation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-hop reasoning chains improve generalization across diverse VQA tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: broad improvements across 24 benchmarks

hypotheses.hypothesis: Full chains are critical vs. single/half-hop variants
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 7.0 point drop with single-hop, 5.3 with half-hop

title: Astrolabe: Steering Forward-Process Reinforcement Learning for Distilled Autoregressive Video Models
task: Reinforcement learning alignment for distilled autoregressive video models
key_results: Improved generation quality across multiple distilled AR models; forward-process RL without reverse-process unrolling
comments: Efficient online RL for video but evaluation metrics and quantitative gains not specified in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Forward-process RL avoids expensive reverse-process overhead
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: streaming training with rolling KV-cache

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-reward objective with uncertainty regularization mitigates reward hacking
hypotheses.result: Inferred confirmed from design but no ablation results in abstract

title: TerraScope: Pixel-Grounded Visual Reasoning for Earth Observation
task: Pixel-grounded geospatial reasoning for earth observation
key_results: Outperforms existing VLMs on pixel-grounded reasoning; 1M-sample Terra-CoT dataset with pixel-level masks
comments: Addresses important niche in multimodal reasoning but benchmark quantitative results not reported in abstract.

inputs: optical image OR SAR image OR both

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-temporal reasoning improves change detection accuracy
hypotheses.result: Inferred design choice but no explicit validation in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: Modality fusion improves reasoning over single modality
hypotheses.result: Inferred capability but quantitative support absent

title: ProactiveBench: Benchmarking Proactiveness in Multimodal Large Language Models
task: Benchmarking proactiveness in multimodal large language models
key_results: MLLMs generally lack proactiveness; RL fine-tuning improves scores and generalizes; conversation history introduces negative bias
comments: Novel benchmark concept but modest improvements (proactiveness remains limited); causality between fine-tuning and generalization not firmly established.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Larger model capacity correlates with higher proactiveness
hypotheses.result: Rejected: proactiveness does not correlate with capacity

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL fine-tuning can learn proactive behavior
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: shows generalization to unseen scenarios

hypotheses.hypothesis: In-context learning and conversation history harm proactiveness
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: introduce negative biases

title: LumosX: Relate Any Identities with Their Attributes for Personalized Video Generation
task: Identity-attribute alignment in personalized video generation
key_results: State-of-the-art performance on fine-grained multi-subject video generation; face-attribute consistency across subjects
comments: Strong model design with relational attention but evaluation against baselines and quantitative metrics missing from abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Relational self/cross-attention enforces intra-group consistency
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design; no ablation results in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: MLLM-inferred subject-attribute priors improve generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred design choice without explicit validation

title: FlowScene: Style-Consistent Indoor Scene Generation with Multimodal Graph Rectified Flow
task: Style-consistent 3D indoor scene generation via multimodal graphs
key_results: Outperforms language and graph-conditioned baselines in realism and style consistency; tri-branch generation for layout/shape/texture
comments: Solid technical approach but abstract lacks quantitative metrics (LPIPS, FID, human preference percentages).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Graph-based modeling ensures scene-level style coherence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed vs. baselines but degree of improvement unspecified

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tight-coupled rectified flow enables collaborative object generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred from architecture; no ablation in abstract

title: The Y-Combinator for LLMs: Solving Long-Context Rot with λ-Calculus
task: Long-context reasoning with typed functional recursion
key_results: 21.9 point improvement on long-context tasks; 4.1x latency reduction; formal termination and cost bounds
comments: Principled approach replacing free-form code with λ-calculus; formal guarantees are valuable but practical bottlenecks of symbolic execution unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Typed functional runtime improves reliability and efficiency over free-form RLM
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 29/36 model-task comparisons favor λ-RLM; +21.9 points average

hypotheses.hypothesis: Formal guarantees (termination, cost bounds) are achievable with λ-calculus
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: theoretical analysis provided

title: Hyperagents
task: Open-ended self-improvement through metacognitive self-modification
key_results: DGM-H outperforms non-self-improving baselines and prior systems; meta-level improvements accumulate and transfer across domains
comments: Ambitious self-referential design but scope limited to code generation domain; transferability to general tasks unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Metacognitive self-modification accelerates improvement beyond standard self-improvement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: outperforms DGM and baselines across domains

hypotheses.hypothesis: Meta-level improvements transfer across domains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: improvements persist in new domains

title: Reasoning as Compression: Unifying Budget Forcing via the Conditional Information Bottleneck
task: Efficient chain-of-thought reasoning with computational budget constraints
key_results: Preserves accuracy at moderate compression while enabling aggressive compression with minimal accuracy drop; identifies theoretical gap in applying Information Bottleneck to transformers
comments: The Conditional Information Bottleneck framing elegantly unifies existing heuristic approaches while introducing semantic cost via surprisal, though empirical validation details are sparse in the abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Naive IB violates Markov property in transformer attention between prompt, reasoning, and response
hypotheses.result: Conditional Information Bottleneck resolves this by modeling reasoning as computational bridge containing only information about response not directly accessible from prompt

hypotheses.hypothesis: Semantic cost priors (surprisal-based) outperform token-counting heuristics
hypotheses.result: CIB approach prunes cognitive bloat while preserving fluency and logic better than length penalties

title: A Subgoal-driven Framework for Improving Long-Horizon LLM Agents
task: Long-horizon web navigation via subgoal decomposition and milestone rewards
key_results: Gemini +10% on WebArena-Lite; Gemma3-12B 43.0% (vs. 6.4% baseline); surpasses GPT-4-Turbo and GPT-4o
comments: Impressive results but WebArena-Lite is a simplified benchmark; generalization to full WebArena or other domains unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Subgoal decomposition improves online planning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 10% improvement on Gemini

hypotheses.hypothesis: Milestone-based rewards outperform sparse rewards in RL
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Gemma3-12B 43.0% vs. 6.4%

title: Deep Tabular Research via Continual Experience-Driven Execution
task: Long-horizon tabular reasoning via hierarchical meta graphs and structured memory
key_results: Multi-step reasoning over interdependent table regions; expectation-aware selection policy with siamese structured memory
comments: Reasonable agentic formulation for tabular QA but benchmark performance numbers and baseline comparisons missing.

model_io.model: Deep Tabular Research agent (meta graph builder + selection policy + memory manager)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical meta graph captures bidirectional table semantics
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design; no validation in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structured siamese memory enables continual refinement
hypotheses.result: Inferred design choice without empirical evidence

title: Versatile Editing of Video Content, Actions, and Dynamics without Training
task: Training-free video editing with action and dynamics modification
key_results: State-of-the-art on complex text-based edits including action changes and object interactions; training-free inversion-free approach
comments: Impressive scope (actions, interactions, global effects) but abstract lacks quantitative comparisons (LPIPS, SSIM, user studies).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Naive inversion-free adaptation causes low-frequency/high-frequency artifacts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: article identifies and addresses mechanisms

hypotheses.hypothesis: Proposed mechanisms overcome alignment and jitter issues
hypotheses.result: Inferred from SOTA claim; specifics not in abstract

title: How Well Does Generative Recommendation Generalize?
task: Analyzing generalization vs. memorization in generative recommendation models
key_results: GR models better on generalization instances, ID-based better on memorization; token-level analysis reveals apparent generalization is token-level memorization
comments: Insightful categorization but the distinction between item- and token-level generalization may oversimplify real patterns.

model_io.model: Generative recommendation (GR) models, item ID-based models
outputs: predicted next item OR generated item sequence

hypotheses.hypothesis: GR models generalize better due to compositional token patterns
hypotheses.result: Partially rejected: token-level analysis shows memorization not true generalization

hypotheses.hypothesis: Memorization and generalization paradigms are complementary
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: adaptive combination improves overall performance

title: WorldAgents: Can Foundation Image Models be Agents for 3D World Models?
task: 3D world synthesis from 2D foundation image models via multi-agent architecture
key_results: 2D models exhibit implicit 3D world understanding; agentic framing enables coherent 3D reconstruction and novel view synthesis
comments: Clever framing of 2D generation as 3D world modeling but quantitative 3D consistency metrics (depth error, reprojection) not in abstract.

model_io.model: VLM director + image generator + VLM verifier

hypotheses.hypothesis: 2D foundation models implicitly encode 3D world structure
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: agentic approach harvests this for coherent 3D synthesis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Two-step VLM verification improves 3D consistency
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design but no ablation results

title: BEAVER: A Training-Free Hierarchical Prompt Compression Method via Structure-Aware Page Selection
task: Training-free hierarchical prompt compression for long contexts
key_results: 26.4x latency reduction on 128k contexts; maintains high multi-needle retrieval fidelity on RULER benchmark
comments: Practical compression method but comparison to LongLLMLingua performance parity suggests marginal gains for the added complexity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structure-aware page selection preserves discourse integrity better than token pruning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: comparable to SOTA with better retrieval fidelity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical pooling enables efficient handling of 128k contexts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 26.4x latency reduction

title: HiMu: Hierarchical Multimodal Frame Selection for Long Video Question Answering
task: Efficient frame selection for long-form video question answering
key_results: 16 frames with Qwen3-VL 8B outperforms competing selectors; 10x fewer FLOPs than agent systems with 32-512 frames
comments: Training-free hierarchical decomposition is elegant but reliance on external experts (CLIP, OCR, ASR) may limit end-to-end reasoning.

model_io.model: HiMu (LLM query decomposer + expert routers + fuzzy logic composer)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical logic tree decomposition recovers sub-event structure better than similarity matching
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: outperforms dense-vector selectors

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lightweight expert routing is more efficient than iterative LVLM calls
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 10x FLOP reduction vs. agent methods

title: LoopRPT: Reinforcement Pre-Training for Looped Language Models
task: Reinforcement pre-training for looped language models with latent reasoning
key_results: Improved per-step representation quality; Pareto-optimal accuracy-computation trade-offs; significant gains on hard tokens
comments: Novel RL formulation for latent computation is principled but absolute accuracy numbers and baseline comparisons not in abstract.

model_io.model: LoopRPT (Ouro looped architecture with latent RL)

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL on latent steps compresses reasoning into fewer iterations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: improved per-step quality

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hard tokens benefit more from latent RL than easy tokens
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: significant gains on hard tokens indicate early-stage reasoning improvement

title: EgoForge: Goal-Directed Egocentric World Simulator
task: Goal-directed egocentric video generation from minimal inputs
key_results: Coherent first-person rollouts from single image + instruction; VideoDiffusionNFT trajectory-level refinement improves intent alignment and temporal consistency
comments: Addresses underexplored egocentric setting but quantitative metrics (LPIPS, semantic alignment scores) and comparison to baselines absent.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trajectory-level reward guidance improves goal alignment in egocentric generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred confirmed from design; no ablation details

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optional exocentric context helps maintain temporal consistency
hypotheses.result: Suggested as optional input but validation not reported

title: Beyond Single Tokens: Distilling Discrete Diffusion Models via Discrete MMD
task: Distillation of discrete diffusion models via moment matching
key_results: Discrete MMD maintains quality and diversity; distilled generators outperform teachers; applicable to text and images
comments: Extends continuous distillation ideas to discrete domain but evaluation on standard benchmarks (perplexity, LPIPS) not detailed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Moment matching prevents quality collapse in discrete distillation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: maintains high quality and diversity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Distilled models can exceed teacher performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: newly distilled generators outperform teachers

title: Cooperation and Exploitation in LLM Policy Synthesis for Sequential Social Dilemmas
task: LLM policy synthesis for multi-agent social dilemmas
key_results: Dense feedback (reward + social metrics) matches or exceeds sparse feedback; LLMs learn cooperative strategies; five reward-hacking attack classes identified
comments: Interesting exploration of feedback design but limited to two canonical games; transferability to real-world cooperation unknown.

model_io.model: LLM policy generator (Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Social metrics serve as coordination signals aiding cooperation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: dense feedback improves cooperation without over-optimization

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs can engage in reward hacking in these environments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: five attack classes identified and characterized

title: AgentDS Technical Report: Benchmarking the Future of Human-AI Collaboration in Domain-Specific Data Science
task: Evaluating AI agents vs. human-AI collaboration on domain-specific data science
key_results: 17 challenges across 6 industries; AI-only near/below median; strongest results from human-AI collaboration
comments: Important benchmark highlighting domain expertise value, but open competition results may suffer from variable team quality rather than inherent AI limitations.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current AI agents struggle with domain-specific reasoning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: AI-only baselines near/below median

hypotheses.hypothesis: Human-AI collaboration outperforms AI-only approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: strongest solutions from collaboration

title: Do VLMs Need Vision Transformers? Evaluating State Space Models as Vision Encoders
task: Evaluating state space models as vision encoders for VLMs
key_results: SSM vision backbones competitive or superior to ViT under matched initialization; maintain strong performance at smaller scale; stabilization strategies improve robustness
comments: Systematic evaluation suggests SSM viability but practical adoption hindered by transformer ecosystem dominance and inference hardware optimization.

model_io.model: VLM (SSM backbone or ViT backbone + LLM connector)

hypotheses.hypothesis: SSM backbones match or exceed ViT performance in VLMs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: strongest overall performance on VQA/grounding

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dense-task tuning improves both backbone families
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: detection/segmentation training beneficial

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher ImageNet accuracy does not guarantee VLM gains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: backbone scale/accuracy poorly predictive of VLM performance

title: Language on Demand, Knowledge at Core: Composing LLMs with Encoder-Decoder Translation Models for Extensible Multilinguality
task: Compositional encoder-LLM-decoder for extensible multilinguality
key_results: Outperforms baselines on multilingual tasks; especially strong on low-resource and unseen languages; no LLM retraining required
comments: Pragmatic compositional approach but cross-model alignment complexity and inference latency overhead not discussed.

model_io.model: XBridge (encoder + LLM + decoder with alignment layers)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pretrained encoder-decoder models complement LLM multilingual weaknesses
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: improvements across four LLMs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lightweight alignment layers bridge model representation misalignment
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design; quantitative impact not reported

title: CurveStream: Boosting Streaming Video Understanding in MLLMs via Curvature-Aware Hierarchical Visual Memory Management
task: Streaming video understanding in MLLMs via curvature-aware memory management
key_results: 10.69% gain on StreamingBench, 13.58% on OVOBench; 26.4x latency reduction on 128k contexts; curvature-based semantic transition detection
comments: Practical training-free approach but relies on geometric insight (curvature ↔ semantic transitions) that may not generalize across all video types.

model_io.model: CurveStream (curvature scorer + K-Sigma dynamic threshold + memory manager)

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-curvature regions align with critical semantic transitions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: guides effective frame retention

hypotheses.hypothesis: Curvature-aware selection outperforms uniform sampling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 10%+ gains on benchmarks

title: Teaching an Agent to Sketch One Part at a Time
task: Vector sketch generation one part at a time via multi-turn RL
key_results: ControlSketch-Part dataset with part-level annotations; interpretable and locally-editable sketch generation; multi-turn process-reward RL
comments: Addresses controllability but abstract lacks quantitative metrics (LPIPS, perceptual similarity, user studies) and baseline comparisons.

model_io.model: Multimodal language model agent with process-reward RL

hypotheses.hypothesis: Part-level annotations enable controllable generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design and dataset; quantitative support absent

hypotheses.hypothesis: Visual feedback during generation improves interpretability
hypotheses.result: Inferred capability but validation not detailed

title: DROID-SLAM in the Wild
task: Real-time RGB SLAM in dynamic environments
key_results: State-of-the-art camera poses and geometry in cluttered dynamic scenarios at ~10 FPS; handles unknown dynamic objects via per-pixel uncertainty estimation
comments: The reliance on multi-view visual feature inconsistency for uncertainty estimation is sensible, but real-world generalization to truly unconstrained scenarios remains to be demonstrated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-view visual feature inconsistency enables robust per-pixel uncertainty estimation
hypotheses.result: System achieves state-of-the-art tracking and reconstruction in dynamic, cluttered scenes in real time

title: Probing Cultural Signals in Large Language Models through Author Profiling
task: Probing cultural biases in LLMs through author profiling from lyrics
key_results: Non-trivial profiling performance with systematic cultural alignment; Ministral-8B shows strongest ethnicity bias, Gemma-12B most balanced; introduces fairness metrics MAD and RD
comments: Zero-shot author profiling is a clever proxy for cultural encoding, though the causal mechanism linking lyrics to demographic inferences remains unclear.

model_io.model: Multiple open-source LLMs (DeepSeek-1.5B, Ministral-8B, Gemma-12B, etc.)

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs encode systematic cultural biases detectable via author profiling
hypotheses.result: Models demonstrate non-trivial profiling performance with clear cultural alignment patterns (North American default, Asian alignment in DeepSeek)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fairness metrics MAD and RD capture ethnicity bias disparities across models
hypotheses.result: Metrics successfully differentiate bias levels, with Ministral-8B highest and Gemma-12B most balanced

title: ReLi3D: Relightable Multi-view 3D Reconstruction with Disentangled Illumination
task: Unified 3D reconstruction with geometry, material, and illumination estimation
key_results: End-to-end pipeline reconstructs complete 3D assets in under one second; generalizable across synthetic PBR and real-world RGB captures
comments: The two-path prediction strategy elegantly decouples object appearance from environment illumination, though computational requirements and scalability to complex scenes warrant scrutiny.

model_io.model: Transformer cross-conditioning architecture with two-path predictor
Differentiable Monte Carlo multiple importance sampling renderer

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-view constraints dramatically improve material and illumination disentanglement versus single-image methods
hypotheses.result: Unified end-to-end pipeline achieves near-instantaneous generation of relightable 3D assets with improved accuracy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mixed domain training (synthetic + real-world) improves generalization
hypotheses.result: Generalizable results across geometry, material accuracy, and illumination quality

title: TAPESTRY: From Geometry to Appearance via Consistent Turntable Videos
task: Texture synthesis and reconstruction for untextured 3D models via turntable video generation
key_results: High-fidelity 360-degree turntable videos with strict geometric consistency; 3D reconstruction from TTV; multi-stage inpainting for self-occluded regions
comments: Clever geometry-conditioned video diffusion but abstract lacks quantitative metrics (chamfer distance, texture quality) and comparison to texture synthesis baselines.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Geometry-conditioned diffusion maintains 3D consistency better than unconditioned generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred confirmed from design; quantitative validation not in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-stage inpainting effectively handles self-occlusion
hypotheses.result: Inferred from pipeline design; metric results absent

title: ReLMXEL: Adaptive RL-Based Memory Controller with Explainable Energy and Latency Optimization
task: Dynamic memory controller optimization for latency and energy efficiency
key_results: Consistent performance gains over baseline configurations driven by workload-specific memory access behavior; incorporates explainability via reward decomposition
comments: Multi-agent RL for memory control is well-motivated, but the abstract lacks concrete metrics (latency reduction %, energy savings %) and comparison baselines.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reward decomposition enables explainable memory controller optimization
hypotheses.result: ReLMXEL achieves consistent performance gains with improved transparency of control decisions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Workload-specific memory access patterns drive optimization refinements
hypotheses.result: Performance gains vary with workload characteristics, suggesting adaptive behavior

title: Human-AI Synergy in Agentic Code Review
task: Comparative analysis of human and AI agent effectiveness in code review
key_results: Human reviewers provide 11.8% more rounds on AI-generated code; AI suggestions adopted at significantly lower rate than human suggestions; AI suggestions cause larger code complexity/size increases
comments: This large-scale empirical study reveals critical gaps in AI agent code review—adoption rates and suggestion quality matter more than scale—highlighting the necessity of human oversight.

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI agents and human reviewers provide different types of feedback
hypotheses.result: Human reviewers provide additional feedback on understanding, testing, and knowledge transfer; humans exchange more review rounds for AI-generated code

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI agent suggestions are lower quality than human suggestions
hypotheses.result: AI suggestions adopted at significantly lower rate; over half of unadopted AI suggestions are incorrect or address via alternative fixes; adopted AI suggestions cause larger complexity increases

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI agents can scale defect screening but lack contextual judgment
hypotheses.result: Human oversight remains critical for suggestion quality and contextual feedback

title: s2n-bignum-bench: A practical benchmark for evaluating low-level code reasoning of LLMs
task: Low-level code reasoning in cryptographic assembly via formal verification
key_results: s2n-bignum-bench: first public benchmark for HOL Light proof synthesis on industrial crypto routines; practical and formally verified testbed
comments: Valuable contribution for neurosymbolic reasoning but benchmark difficulty and expected baseline performance not characterized.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Competition-math success does not transfer to industrial code verification
hypotheses.result: Motivation for benchmark; specific performance not provided in abstract

title: Self-reorganization and information transfer in large-scale models of fish schools
task: Investigate how flow interactions and group size affect collective cohesion, responsiveness, and information transfer in fish schools
key_results: Flow interactions destabilize schools into constantly fragmenting clusters; correlation length decreases before fragmentation events, weakening collective responsiveness; directional information propagates linearly in time via non-reciprocal visual interactions
comments: Computational study of 50,000 individuals elegantly connects hydrodynamics to behavioral transitions, though validation against empirical fish schooling data would strengthen claims.

model_io.model: Computational fish school model (up to 50,000 individuals)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Scale-free correlations are group size-independent in cohesive schools
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in computational models; fragmentation events preceded by decreased correlation length

hypotheses.hypothesis: Information about directional changes propagates linearly due to non-reciprocal visual interactions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; merging speeds up, fragmentation slows down information transfer

title: Mechanisms of active wetting and fluidification in epithelial cell collectives
task: Elucidate molecular and biophysical mechanisms regulating epithelial fluidity and wetting transitions in breast cancer
key_results: IRSp53 depletion increases breast cancer cell fluidity and active wetting by reducing intercellular friction; IRSp53–Afadin interaction controls global tensile state; low IRSp53 expression correlates with worse clinical outcomes in patient samples
comments: Well-integrated study linking single-protein perturbations to tissue-scale phase transitions and clinical phenotypes, though mechanistic details of IRSp53–Afadin crosstalk remain incompletely characterized.

model_io.model: Breast cancer epithelial collectives (2D monolayers and 3D spheroids)

hypotheses.hypothesis: IRSp53 regulates epithelial fluidity through control of intercellular friction and cell rearrangements
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; IRSp53 depletion increases fluidity via decreased friction and enhanced rearrangements

hypotheses.hypothesis: IRSp53–Afadin interaction controls global tensile state and active wetting
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; this interaction identified as key regulator of epithelial viscosity

title: Generating 3D models of complex carbohydrates with GLYCAM-Web
task: Provide online tools for 3D structure prediction of glycans and polysaccharides
key_results: GLYCAM-Web tools generate experimentally consistent 3D structures of oligosaccharides, glycoproteins, glycosaminoglycans, and engineered glycomaterials; integrated with external databases like the Protein Data Bank
comments: Useful infrastructure tool with broad scope, but abstract provides no quantitative validation metrics or accuracy benchmarks against experimental structures.

title: Multifunctional metallic nanomushrooms on nanowires for detecting and killing tumor cells
task: Synthesize multifunctional nanomaterials for simultaneous tumor cell detection and photothermal ablation
key_results: Metallic alloy nanomushrooms on nanowires demonstrated enhanced fluorescence visualization and photothermal cell killing in a single theranostic agent; superior electrical conductivity improved photothermal conversion efficiency
comments: While the material engineering is solid, quantitative efficacy metrics (kill rates, detection sensitivity) are not provided in the abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unique mushroom morphology enables dense targeting molecule loading
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through simultaneous tumor visualization and elimination

title: pan-ASLM: Axially Swept Light Sheet Microscopy for Fast and High-Resolution Imaging of Expanded Samples
task: Develop high-resolution light-sheet microscopy for imaging expanded cellular and tissue samples
key_results: pan-ASLM achieved 586 nm lateral and 428 nm axial resolution over 640×640 µm² field of view at 20 fps (183 Mvoxels/sec), providing ~1200× faster imaging and ~2× better axial resolution than confocal microscopy on expanded samples
comments: Excellent technical achievement enabling nanoscale imaging of large tissue volumes, though biological validation is limited to standard cell lines and normal tissues.

outputs: nanoscale cellular ultrastructure at 586 nm lateral / 428 nm axial resolution

title: Visinin-like protein 1 disrupts calcium homeostasis and promotes atrial fibrillation in human and rodent models
task: Identify VILIP-1 as critical mediator of calcium dysregulation and atrial fibrillation via NCX-1 trafficking
key_results: VILIP-1 upregulated in AF patients and pacing-induced AF models; cardiomyocyte overexpression causes pathological Ca²⁺ leakage, DADs, APD alternans; VILIP-1 augments NCX-1 surface abundance via myristoylation-dependent trafficking; FDA-approved drugs (repaglinide, desloratadine) attenuate AF
comments: Comprehensive mechanistic pathway from single protein to arrhythmia phenotype with rapid drug repositioning, though patient efficacy data remains absent.

model_io.model: AF patient atrial tissue, cardiomyocyte-specific VILIP-1 overexpression mice, pacing-induced AF models

hypotheses.hypothesis: VILIP-1 upregulation disrupts atrial calcium handling
hypotheses.result: Cardiomyocyte overexpression causes pathological Ca²⁺ leakage and DADs

hypotheses.hypothesis: VILIP-1 enhances NCX-1 trafficking via myristoylation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; myristoylation inhibitors reduce NCX-1 surface expression and restore calcium homeostasis

title: Osteosarcoma cells promote intracellular iron detoxification to mitigate GPX4-mediated ferroptosis
task: Characterize ferroptosis resistance mechanisms in osteosarcoma via GPX4 inhibition adaptation
key_results: GPX4 inhibition (RSL3) triggers NRF2 activation and compensatory antioxidant response; increases ferritin/ferroportin promoting iron depletion; activates HIF-1α/c-MET/MSPR survival pathways; combination therapy enhances cytotoxicity
comments: Comprehensive characterization of adaptive resistance mechanisms, though specific combination partners for optimal ferroptosis sensitization remain to be determined.

model_io.model: Osteosarcoma cell lines (U2OS, MG-63, HOS, Saos2, 143B)

hypotheses.hypothesis: GPX4 inhibition activates NRF2-driven compensatory antioxidant response
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; AKR1C1/HO-1/NQO1 upregulation observed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Iron homeostasis disruption confers ferroptosis resistance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ferritin/ferroportin upregulation promotes iron depletion

title: DR5/WDR12 balances p65 stability promoting sunitinib resistance in renal cell carcinoma
task: Characterize DR5/WDR12/p65 positive feedback loop in sunitinib resistance in renal cell carcinoma
key_results: DR5 upregulated in ccRCC and sunitinib-resistant cells; DR5 reduces WDR12-mediated p65 ubiquitination via competitive E3 ligase binding; DR5-p65 feedback upregulates BCL2 conferring resistance; targeting DR5/NF-κB/BCL2 axis sensitizes to sunitinib
comments: Multi-level mechanistic analysis of resistance feedback loop is thorough, though clinical validation of combination strategy remains preliminary.

model_io.model: ccRCC cell lines, patient samples, xenografts

hypotheses.hypothesis: DR5 competitively binds WDR12 to protect p65 from degradation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via co-immunoprecipitation and ubiquitination assays

hypotheses.hypothesis: DR5-p65 positive feedback maintains sunitinib resistance
hypotheses.result: Targeting DR5/NF-κB/BCL2 axis sensitizes in vitro and in vivo

title: Dysfunction of α2δ4 leads to photoreceptor degeneration through disrupted synaptic mitochondria and calcium crosstalk
task: Link synaptic mitochondrial dysfunction to photoreceptor degeneration via α2δ4-mediated calcium crosstalk
key_results: α2δ4 loss causes late-onset photoreceptor degeneration (~7 months); synaptic mitochondria reduced in size/number; TCA cycle downregulated; RyR2/MCU downregulated in outer plexiform layer
comments: Compelling organellar imaging data, but mechanistic link between early calcium disruption and late degeneration remains incompletely resolved.

model_io.model: α2δ4 KO mice, Cav1.4 KO mice, human retinal cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: α2δ4 loss impairs Cav1.4 function
hypotheses.result: Cav1.4 KO shows earlier degeneration than α2δ4 KO

hypotheses.hypothesis: Disrupted calcium crosstalk damages synaptic mitochondria
hypotheses.result: Reduced mitochondrial size, number, and proteins in OPL; RyR2/MCU downregulation

title: Noncanonical role of KDM5C in conferring bortezomib resistance via the PERK‒Nrf2 axis in multiple myeloma
task: Elucidate noncanonical KDM5C role in bortezomib resistance via demethylase-independent mechanism
key_results: KDM5C-CBP-MYC complex activates PERK via H3K27ac (not H3K4me); Nrf2 phosphorylation enhances unfolded protein response; KDM5C depletion sensitizes MM to bortezomib
comments: Elegant dissection of noncanonical function, but mechanism relies heavily on complex formation; direct PERK activation validation would strengthen claims.

model_io.model: MM cell lines, patient samples, xenografts

hypotheses.hypothesis: KDM5C acts via demethylase-independent mechanism in MM
hypotheses.result: H3K4me status unchanged; H3K27ac is key acetylation mark

hypotheses.hypothesis: KDM5C-CBP-MYC complex activates PERK promoter
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via chromatin immunoprecipitation and reporter assays

title: Dual targeting of PI3Kδ and PPARα enhances antitumor activity via FoxO1 activation in follicular lymphoma
task: Overcome adaptive resistance to PI3Kδ inhibition in follicular lymphoma via PPARα co-targeting
key_results: Combination of linperlisib + chiglitazar achieves superior tumor control in PDX models; FoxO1 reactivation drives treatment response
comments: Mechanistic rationale is solid, but clinical efficacy remains untested; FoxO1 biomarker nomination strengthens translational potential.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PI3Kδ and PPARα inhibition have complementary metabolic effects
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via metabolic and signaling pathway analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: FoxO1 is essential for combination efficacy
hypotheses.result: FoxO1 depletion attenuates treatment response

title: IFI16 is essential to linking DNA damage and ferroptosis in acute kidney injury
task: Link DNA damage response to ferroptosis in acute kidney injury via IFI16
key_results: IFI16/p204 elevated in AKI patients and I/R-induced models; p204 deficiency ameliorates TEC ferroptosis; IFI16 enhances PARP-1 PARylation, activating ATM-p53 signaling driving ferroptosis
comments: Clear mechanistic pathway from DNA damage to ferroptosis, but therapeutic targeting of IFI16 in clinical AKI remains untested.

model_io.model: Renal ischemia/reperfusion mouse model, HK-2 cells, patient ATN samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: IFI16 links DNA damage to ferroptosis via PARP-1 interaction
hypotheses.result: p204 deficiency reduces ferroptosis; IFI16 KO in HK-2 cells ameliorates damage

hypotheses.hypothesis: IFI16 HIN and PYRIN domains are required
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; domain-dependent DDR activation

title: SARS-CoV-2 nonspike structural proteins hijack mucosa epithelial cell fate
task: Determine SARS-CoV-2 nonspike protein-induced epithelial dedifferentiation mechanism via CNN2
key_results: Envelope protein induces epithelial dedifferentiation, micronuclei, G1 arrest, apoptosis; CNN2 elevation in COVID-19 epithelium; CNN2 downregulation inhibits apoptosis and promotes differentiation
comments: Identifies CNN2 as actionable target with clinical relevance, though 'double hijack' mechanism (GLIS2-CNN2-differentiation disruption) requires further mechanistic validation.

model_io.model: COVID-19 patient mucosal epithelia, in vitro epithelial culture models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nonspike proteins induce epithelial dedifferentiation via CNN2
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CNN2 levels elevated in COVID-19 epithelium

hypotheses.hypothesis: GLIS2 negatively regulates CNN2
hypotheses.result: Supported; GLIS2 associated with ciliary dysfunction disruption

title: HNF4α-HKDC1 axis orchestrates a metabolic rewiring to promote migration and metastasis in advanced gastric cancer
task: Elucidate HNF4α-HKDC1 axis driving metabolic rewiring in metastatic gastric cancer
key_results: P2-HNF4α highly expressed in GC metastasis; directly binds HKDC1 enhancer; mycophenolic acid antagonizes HNF4α and suppresses migration/metastasis in vivo
comments: Integrated omics approach is robust, but therapeutic efficacy of mycophenolic acid in clinical GC remains untested.

hypotheses.hypothesis: P2-HNF4α activates HKDC1 via enhancer binding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via ChIP; upregulation drives metabolic rewiring

hypotheses.hypothesis: Glycolytic pathway supports GC cell migration
hypotheses.result: Integrated transcriptome-metabolome analysis confirms involvement

title: Deciphering the STAT3-PXN positive feedback loop in GBM, IDH-wildtype: transcriptional regulation and inhibition of YB-1 ubiquitination
task: Decipher STAT3-PXN feedback loop and YB-1 stabilization in GBM prognosis
key_results: PXN upregulated in IDH-wildtype GBM; STAT3↔PXN positive feedback; PXN stabilizes YB-1 via ubiquitination inhibition
comments: Comprehensive mechanistic dissection but lacks direct therapeutic validation or in vivo efficacy data.

hypotheses.hypothesis: STAT3 and PXN form positive feedback loop
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via transcriptional and protein interaction assays

hypotheses.hypothesis: PXN inhibits YB-1 ubiquitination
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; YB-1 drives downstream malignancy pathways

title: Dynamic changes of the immune microenvironment in ovarian cancer following neoadjuvant chemotherapy
task: Identify immunosuppressive mechanisms of chemotherapy resistance in ovarian cancer
key_results: Prostaglandins mediate MDSC formation post-cisplatin; combination with prostaglandin inhibitor restores CD8+ T cell cytotoxicity and improves efficacy
comments: Pathway identified via scRNA-seq is mechanistically plausible, but in vivo validation limited to xenograft models.

model_io.model: Ovarian cancer cell lines, xenografts; scRNA-seq from patient samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cisplatin induces prostaglandin-mediated MDSC formation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in vitro and in vivo

hypotheses.hypothesis: Prostaglandin inhibition restores T cell function
hypotheses.result: Combination therapy improves efficacy vs. cisplatin alone

title: Social status impacts T-cell responses through synapse strength in the prefrontal cortex
task: Link social status to immune function via prefrontal cortex synaptic strength
key_results: Second-ranked mice show superior T-cell responses to vaccination; increased dmPFC synaptic strength is sufficient to boost antigen-specific T-cell percentages; causal dmPFC-peripheral immune link established
comments: Innovative neuroimmune circuit demonstration, but small sample sizes and male-only cohorts limit generalizability to other social contexts and sexes.

model_io.model: Social hierarchy mouse groups, dmPFC manipulation (optogenetic/chemogenetic)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Social status modulates T-cell responses via dmPFC circuits
hypotheses.result: Second-ranked mice show superior T-cell responses

hypotheses.hypothesis: dmPFC synaptic strength causally drives T-cell responses
hypotheses.result: Selective dmPFC synapse or neuron activation boosts T-cell percentages

title: Maturase K forms a plastidial splicing complex with a neofunctionalized branching enzyme
task: Identify splicing complex components and mechanism for chloroplast group IIA introns
key_results: MatK forms heteromultimeric complex with MKIP1 (neofunctionalized branching enzyme); MKIP1 silencing reduces MatK intron splicing by ~80%
comments: Novel functional divergence of enzyme reveals protein evolution mechanisms, though in vitro reconstitution of splicing activity would strengthen claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MKIP1 lost BE catalytic activity and gained MatK-interaction domain
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via sequence analysis and binding studies

hypotheses.hypothesis: MKIP1 facilitates general splicing factor function
hypotheses.result: MKIP1 silencing suppresses multiple MatK target splicing

title: Sex-specific KDM6A-HNF4A-CREBH network controls lipoprotein cholesterol metabolism and atherosclerosis via epigenetic reprograming of hepatocytes
task: Determine sex-specific KDM6A-HNF4A-CREBH network in hepatic cholesterol metabolism
key_results: KDM6A essential in female hepatocytes; females lacking hepatic KDM6A develop pro-atherogenic lipoproteins and increased atherosclerosis; males largely unaffected; KDM6A-HNF4A enable CREBH-dependent transcription
comments: Sex-specific epigenetic mechanism is novel and well-validated, though mechanistic details of X-linkage dominance effects remain incomplete.

model_io.model: Female and male mice with hepatocyte-specific KDM6A deletion, human liver cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: KDM6A is essential in female hepatocytes for cholesterol metabolism
hypotheses.result: Female KO mice develop pro-atherogenic phenotype; males unaffected

hypotheses.hypothesis: KDM6A cooperates with HNF4A-CREBH for lipid gene expression
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via chromatin activation and transcription assays

title: Engineering NK and T cells with metabolite-sensing receptors to target solid tumors
task: Engineer metabolite-sensing receptors in NK and T cells to enhance tumor infiltration
key_results: CRISPR screen identifies GPR183, GPR84, GPR34, GPR18 as infiltration enhancers; GPR183 expression increases NK, CAR NK, and CAR T cell tumor infiltration and control; alters NK transcriptome in ligand-dependent manner
comments: Innovative CRISPR platform successfully identifies targetable receptors with functional validation, though mechanism of cancer-cell factor sensing requires further characterization.

model_io.model: NK-92 cells, primary NK and T cells, CAR NK/T cells, breast/ovarian cancer models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Metabolite-sensing GPCRs enhance NK/T cell migration to solid tumors
hypotheses.result: GPR183 expression increases infiltration in NK, CAR NK, CAR T cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: GPCR signaling reprograms immune cell transcriptome
hypotheses.result: GPR183 expression alters NK transcriptome in ligand-dependent manner

title: Germ cells are essential for testicular morphogenesis and functional reconstruction in a porcine xenograft model
task: Determine germ cell role in testicular morphogenesis and spermatogenesis in xenograft
key_results: Germ cell-containing grafts develop organized seminiferous tubules with multiple spermatogenic stages; germ cell-free grafts remain fragmented and disorganized
comments: Xenograft model elegantly demonstrates germ cell necessity, though cross-species context may limit direct livestock applicability.

model_io.model: Porcine testicular cell xenograft in immunodeficient mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: Germ cells are essential for testicular morphogenesis
hypotheses.result: GF tissues develop organized tubules; FO tissues lack architecture

hypotheses.hypothesis: Germ cells activate spermatogenesis pathways
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via enrichment of cell cycle and spermatogenesis genes in GF

title: The combined inhibitory effect of butaselen and decitabine against lung cancer cells
task: Evaluate combinatorial efficacy of TrxR and DNMT inhibitors against lung cancer
key_results: Butaselen + decitabine synergistically inhibit proliferation, promote apoptosis, suppress migration; enhanced vs. monotherapy; downregulate DNMT1, reduce Bcl-2/Bax, upregulate HOXA9/p21/E-cadherin
comments: Mechanistically rationalized combination is promising, but limited to 2D cell culture; in vivo efficacy data essential for clinical translation.

model_io.model: Human lung cancer cell lines (A549, H1299)

hypotheses.hypothesis: TrxR and DNMT inhibition have complementary effects
hypotheses.result: Synergistic cytotoxicity at specific ratios and time conditions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined treatment restores differentiation and apoptotic capacity
hypotheses.result: HOXA9/p21/E-cadherin upregulation; reduced Bcl-2/Bax ratio

title: Optochemical elucidation of a critical role of the incomplete spindle assembly checkpoint in zebrafish development
task: Assess spindle assembly checkpoint functionality during zebrafish development using photochemistry
key_results: CENP-E inhibition causes mitotic errors; SAC is leaky but functional in gastrula (partial error correction); SAC suppression renders gastrula embryos inviable upon CENP-E inhibition
comments: Innovative optochemical approach elegantly reveals stage-specific SAC maturation, though causality between error correction and developmental tolerance remains correlative.

hypotheses.hypothesis: SAC functionality increases during gastrulation
hypotheses.result: Gastrula embryos tolerate hours of CENP-E inhibition; pre-gastrula show graded defects

hypotheses.hypothesis: Leaky SAC provides partial error correction
hypotheses.result: SAC suppression renders gastrula inviable with CENP-E inhibition

title: PLIN5 phosphorylation orchestrates mitochondria lipid-droplet coupling to control hepatic lipid flux and steatosis
task: Determine PLIN5 phosphorylation role in mitochondria-LD coupling and hepatic lipid flux
key_results: Fasting increases mitochondria-LD contacts and PLIN5 levels; S155A variant enhances contacts and LD expansion; S155E variant reduces contacts; S155A overexpression in WD reduces lipotoxicity
comments: Novel scPhenomics spatial imaging provides high-resolution organellar interaction map, though in vivo metabolic validation of S155A therapeutic benefit remains limited.

model_io.model: Single-cell tissue imaging (scPhenomics), spatial proteomics; mice under fasting and Western diet

hypotheses.hypothesis: PLIN5 phosphorylation state regulates mitochondria-LD tethering
hypotheses.result: S155A enhances contacts; S155E reduces contacts

hypotheses.hypothesis: Organellar coupling adapts hepatocytes to nutrient stress
hypotheses.result: Fasting increases contacts; WD attenuates coupling; S155A overexpression reduces WD lipotoxicity

title: Integrative structural and physicochemical characterization of chalcone synthase enzymes from medicinal plants using AlphaFold, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics
task: Comparative structural and functional characterization of chalcone synthase from medicinal plants
key_results: CHS adopts conserved thiolase-fold across 13 species; species-specific variations in substrate-binding channel; MD simulations confirm conformational stability over 100 ns
comments: Entirely in silico study with good methodological integration (sequence, structure prediction, docking, dynamics), but lacks experimental validation and actual enzyme kinetic comparisons.

inputs: CHS protein sequences from 13 medicinal plants + Arabidopsis thaliana
outputs: conserved motifs (catalytic triad, GFGPG, catalytic loop)

hypotheses.hypothesis: CHS substrate-binding channel architecture varies across species while maintaining catalytic function
hypotheses.result: Species-specific variations in channel structure observed; functional relevance proposed but unvalidated

hypotheses.hypothesis: Predicted AlphaFold structures and crystal structures show similar ligand-binding stability
hypotheses.result: MD simulations confirm conformational stability for both model sources with favorable MM-PBSA binding energetics

title: In-silico identification of genetic variants associated with chronic lymphocytic leukemia for diagnostic and therapeutic applications
task: Identify genetic variants and key genes associated with chronic lymphocytic leukemia
key_results: 2357 significant SNPs mapped to 40 genomic loci; 7 key genes identified (TERT, BCL2L11, FAS, BCL2, CFLAR, CASP8, LEF1); 4 candidate drug agents proposed
comments: Meta-analysis of GWAS is powerful but results rely on existing databases; functional validation of proposed drugs and genes in CLL models is needed.

outputs: 7 key genes (TERT, BCL2L11, FAS, BCL2, CFLAR, CASP8, LEF1)

hypotheses.hypothesis: CLL susceptibility loci regulate apoptotic and B-cell signaling pathways
hypotheses.result: Key genes enriched in apoptosis (FAS, CASP8, BCL2, BCL2L11) and LEF1 (Wnt signaling)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Identified key genes are druggable targets
hypotheses.result: All 7 genes show known drug-gene interactions; 4 candidate agents proposed

title: Cleavage region organizes the structural architecture of the SINE-derived B2 repressive ribozyme
task: Determine structure-function relationships of B2 SINE ribozyme
key_results: B2 cleavage region is critical for ribozymatic activity and POL-II repression; structural ensemble depends on cleavage domain integrity
comments: Chemical probing and SAXS provide good experimental grounding, though the dynamic structural model remains ensemble-based rather than atomic-resolution.

hypotheses.hypothesis: B2 cleavage domain confers both ribozymatic activity and POL-II repressive function
hypotheses.result: Genetic perturbations of cleavage region reduce both self-cleavage and repression activity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structural integrity of critical region is required for biofunctionality
hypotheses.result: Domain deletions and point mutations disrupt structural ensemble and reduce biological activity

title: Compressed deepfake detection via GA-LASSO selection of deep features and machine learning models
task: Deepfake detection via GA-LASSO feature selection
key_results: >90% feature reduction (2048→120–170 ResNet50 features); FF++ C23 AUC 99.48%, Celeb-DF C0 88.7% accuracy; robust under C40 compression; GA+LASSO comparable to GA with lower computational cost
comments: Hybrid feature selection elegantly balances sparsity and performance; strong generalization under compression and cross-dataset scenarios; practical deployment benefit.

model_io.model: KNN, SVM classifiers on selected features

hypotheses.hypothesis: GA-LASSO joint feature selection reduces dimensionality >90% while maintaining accuracy comparable to GA
hypotheses.result: AUC 99.48 (FF++ C23), 78.74 (Celeb-DF C40); lower computational cost than baseline GA

title: Blockchain-empowered unified services for streamlined traceability implementation in food supply chain: a demonstration in Australian seafood traceability
task: Blockchain-enabled traceability for food supply chain
key_results: Framework demonstrates reliable traceability with minimal integration effort on Australian seafood scenario; prototype deployed with smart contracts; supports interoperability and data integrity
comments: Unified blockchain services architecture addresses adoption barriers (complexity, cost, fragmentation); regulatory-body participation potential noted; scalability and cost-effectiveness claimed but not quantified.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Standardized blockchain services framework reduces integration complexity and cost vs. fragmented implementations
hypotheses.result: Australian seafood prototype validates framework feasibility and interoperability

title: AI-driven data-efficient estimation of partition functions in disordered materials
task: Partition function estimation in disordered materials
key_results: PULSE enables partition function calculation without pre-training datasets at fraction of Monte Carlo cost; generates representative configurations for interatomic potential training
comments: Novel unsupervised generative approach addresses a genuine computational bottleneck in materials science.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Inverse VAE can efficiently sample configuration space without expensive Monte Carlo or incomplete SQS methods
hypotheses.result: Demonstrated on uranium-plutonium mixed oxides; provides physical insight into defect behavior

title: A stacking ensemble with Pareto optimization for scalable electricity theft detection via hybrid data repair and lightweight deployment
task: Electricity theft detection from smart meter data
key_results: ROC-AUC 0.9869, F1-score 94.47%; lightweight variant (STL-Lite) achieves ROC-AUC 0.9858 with 40% latency reduction
comments: Pareto-optimized ensemble combining performance and complexity is pragmatic; SHAP interpretability aids regulatory adoption.

model_io.model: STL-Net: Stacking ensemble (NGBoost, CatBoost, LightGBM, XGBoost)

hypotheses.hypothesis: NSGA-II Pareto optimization yields models with jointly optimal performance and inference cost
hypotheses.result: STL-Lite preserves detection accuracy while reducing latency by ~40%

hypotheses.hypothesis: Weighted attribute normalization effectively standardizes engagement, difficulty, and time allocation for prediction
hypotheses.result: Integrated framework outperforms ML, ensemble, and DL baselines across metrics

title: Defining synergy for three-phase polymer nanocomposites: a volume-weighted quantitative framework
task: Quantify synergy in three-phase polymer nanocomposites
key_results: Modified volume-weighted synergy formulations distinguish cooperative, asymmetric, and inhibiting interactions; reclassify antagonistic cases as synergistic
comments: Addresses inconsistency in synergy quantification through rigorous mathematical framework, with broad applicability to multifunctional composites.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Classical synergy equations fail to account for nanoscale filler functionality and unequal volume contributions
hypotheses.result: Proposed modified formulations correctly identify synergy across mechanical, thermal, electrical, and functional properties

title: A security-oriented four-factor spatio-temporal framework for assessing and mitigating traffic congestion risks
task: Assess and mitigate traffic congestion security risk via graph neural networks
key_results: HiST-Graph (Spatio-Temporal GNN) achieves superior predictive accuracy while providing interpretable risk diagnosis; identifies high-risk segments, quantifies vulnerabilities, reveals congestion precursors
comments: Paradigm shift from descriptive to diagnostic: Four-Factor Framework (Hazard, Exposure, Vulnerability, Mitigation) disentangles security drivers; dynamic risk-propagation learning enhances interpretability.

model_io.model: HiST-Graph: Risk-aware Spatio-Temporal Graph Neural Network
inputs: traffic flow, incident, network topology data

hypotheses.hypothesis: Four-Factor risk framework (Hazard, Exposure, Vulnerability, Mitigation Capacity) enables disentanglement of congestion drivers and diagnosis of root causes
hypotheses.result: HiST-Graph achieves superior accuracy and provides interpretable insights; identifies precursor signals to breakdown

title: Subway door fault prediction employing stacking ensemble learning
task: Subway door fault prediction via stacking ensemble
key_results: Stacking ensemble achieves ROC-AUC 0.977, PR-AUC 0.913; optimal threshold yields accuracy 0.937, precision 0.815, recall 0.810, F1 0.812; outperforms ML/DL baselines on imbalanced fault data
comments: Physically constrained data augmentation preserves kinematic consistency; SHAP verification confirms alignment with failure mechanisms; practical for predictive maintenance.

model_io.model: Stacking ensemble (Random Forest + XGBoost base learners, logistic regression meta-learner)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stacking base learners with probabilistic calibration and dynamic F1-optimized thresholding balances precision/recall under severe class imbalance
hypotheses.result: ROC-AUC 0.977, F1 0.812 on imbalanced fault data; SHAP analysis confirms physical consistency

title: Analyzing the digital customer journey: a novel framework for sequential behavior modeling
task: Digital customer journey analysis via LDA/Markov chains
key_results: LDA/HLDA identifies customer journey types; Markov chains capture sequential behavioral patterns; detects irregularities and undesirable outcomes in 120K+ customer HMO mobile app data (5.5M events/day)
comments: Integrates established techniques (LDA, Markov, leakage detection) for multi-granularity journey analysis; topic-based session summarization reduces complexity; unique large-scale healthcare dataset.

inputs: event sequences from mobile app logs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Topic-based session clustering via LDA reduces customer journey variability and enables sequential diagnostics
hypotheses.result: Markov chain analysis on clusters identifies high-risk paths and irregularities undetectable at full granularity

title: GRNN–DP–MPC Co-optimization for predictive energy management in hybrid UAVs
task: Predictive energy management in hybrid UAVs via GRNN-DP-MPC
key_results: GRNN-DP-MPC integrates voltage forecasting, dynamic programming, and model predictive control; reduces battery energy consumption and improves endurance under practical conditions
comments: Three-stage pipeline (GRNN prediction → DP global optimization → MPC real-time control) well-designed; simulation and experimental validation provided.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Predictive GRNN-DP-MPC co-optimization reduces battery energy consumption vs. reactive strategies
hypotheses.result: Simulation and experimental validation confirm improved endurance and reduced battery usage

title: ROI-guided relational YOLO–SegNet transformer for lightweight bone tumor segmentation and classification from X-ray images
task: Bone tumor segmentation and classification from X-ray images
key_results: 98.5% accuracy, 98.57% F1-score, 97% Dice score on 809 X-ray images; 48 ms inference time on RTX 3090
comments: Strong dataset-specific results but lacks external multi-institutional validation, limiting clinical applicability claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ROI-restricted relational attention reduces computational complexity while maintaining long-range context
hypotheses.result: 12.3M parameters vs. UNet/Mask R-CNN, maintaining 98.5% accuracy

title: Multi-point collaborative mobile replica node detection protocol based on key negotiation
task: Replica node detection in mobile wireless sensor networks
key_results: KN-MCDP protocol achieves high detection rate, reduces storage/communication overhead, balances energy, extends network lifetime; multi-point collaboration with key negotiation prevents information leakage
comments: Extends traditional static-WSN detection to mobile WSNs; encryption (digital signature, ternary polynomial, symmetric) and Bloom filters reduce overhead; practical energy-aware design.

model_io.model: KN-MCDP protocol (cluster heads + mobile nodes + base station)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-point collaboration with encrypted information exchange detects replicas in both static and mobile WSN nodes while preserving energy
hypotheses.result: High detection rate; reduced storage/communication overhead; extended network lifetime

title: A smart monocular vision metrology system based on computer for standing long jump
task: Markerless monocular distance measurement for standing long jump
key_results: Vision pipeline achieves 0.71 cm MAE on field tests; ~23 FPS, ~42.7 ms per frame; ablations show 25.07 cm error without athlete handling, 4.35 cm without perspective mapping
comments: Practical system demonstrating centimeter-level accuracy; key-frame selection, homography, heel segmentation critical; minimal calibration enables school deployment.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Homography-based plane mapping with polynomial perspective-edge bias correction enables centimeter-level distance accuracy
hypotheses.result: 0.71 cm MAE; 4.35 cm error degradation without perspective mapping

title: Predicting the performance of a graphene-based patch antenna using a machine learning model for terahertz applications
task: Predict graphene-based patch antenna performance for THz applications
key_results: ANN predicts antenna performance (S11, VSWR, gain, efficiency, radiation pattern) with R² = 0.99 in 0.7 ms; trained on 784 CST simulations
comments: ANN outperforms RF and SVM as surrogate model, dramatically reducing simulation time; enables rapid design exploration in THz antenna space.

inputs: patch length Lp, width Wp; graphene chemical potential μc, relaxation time τ
outputs: S11, VSWR, gain, efficiency, radiation pattern

hypotheses.hypothesis: ANN can learn nonlinear relationships between antenna geometry, graphene properties, and EM responses
hypotheses.result: ANN achieves R² = 0.99 for all outputs; 1000× speedup vs. full EM simulation

title: CR-MSNet: a dual-branch multi-scale attention network for multi-label chest X-ray classification
task: Multi-label chest X-ray disease classification
key_results: CR-MSNet achieves macro-average AUC 0.847 on ChestX-ray14 dataset; dual-branch architecture with cross-attention and parallel multi-scale channel-spatial attention
comments: Dual-branch (global + local) with cross-attention and PMS-CSA module is well-motivated; two-stage training addresses class imbalance pragmatically.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cross-attention enables adaptive interaction between global and local representations for multi-label classification
hypotheses.result: Integrated dual-branch with PMS-CSA achieves macro-AUC 0.847, confirming role of attention-guided feature interaction

title: Data-adaptive pattern-coupled Bayesian compressive sensing for sparse sound field reconstruction
task: Sound field reconstruction via compressive sensing
key_results: Data-adaptive pattern-coupled Bayesian compressive sensing outperforms existing approaches in reconstruction accuracy and noise robustness; induces block-sparse structure reflecting source continuity
comments: Hierarchical Gaussian-Gamma prior with learnable coupling parameters elegantly captures non-uniform correlation; physically meaningful block-sparse outcomes.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Learnable coupling parameters adapt hyperparameter relationships to non-uniform correlation distributions
hypotheses.result: Dynamic adaptation promotes clustering of non-zero coefficients and block-sparse structure reflecting spatial continuity

title: A performance-optimized V2V task offloading framework for real-time vehicular communication
task: V2V task offloading in vehicular edge computing
key_results: Performance-optimized framework achieves 27% system delay improvement, 18% packet delivery ratio gain, 24% task completion ratio increase vs. baselines; cumulative-score dynamic priority queuing manages high density/mobility
comments: Two-stage design (context-aware edge selection + priority queuing) pragmatic; tunable priority weights adapt to application requirements; OMNeT++/Veins/SUMO validation.

inputs: vehicle topology (speed, distance), task properties (size, priority)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cumulative score weighting (relative speed, distance, task size, priority) improves task completion vs. simple distance-based offloading
hypotheses.result: 27% system delay reduction, 24% task completion gain under high density/mobility

title: A CNN–Bi-LSTM pipeline and open FSW dataset for freestyle wrestling action recognition
task: Freestyle wrestling action recognition
key_results: DeepLabV3+ (foreground) + EfficientNet-B7 + Bi-LSTM achieves 82.9% top-1 accuracy on 210-clip Open FSW dataset (7 techniques, 30 clips/class)
comments: Foreground segmentation adds latency but improves accuracy especially on high-occlusion techniques; modest dataset size limits generalization; good reproducibility with released code.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Foreground-aware segmentation reduces occlusion noise and improves action recognition in close-contact sports
hypotheses.result: Consistent gains for strongest backbone; largest improvements on high-occlusion techniques, at cost of segmentation latency

title: Ancient architecture image classification with progressive stacking pseudoinverse learning
task: Ancient architecture image classification
key_results: AAPSP model shows strong performance on six Chinese architecture datasets in accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score
comments: Progressive stacking and Gaussian/uniform weight initialization for semantic features is domain-specific; limited quantitative detail provided in abstract.

model_io.model: AAPSP: Key Features Stacking Pseudoinverse (KFSP) + Progressive Optimization Learning (POL)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Distribution-aware weight initialization (Gaussian for roofs, uniform for patterns) and attention mechanisms improve feature focus
hypotheses.result: KFSP better captures roof contour and decorative patterns than random initialization

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic sample screening prioritizes rare-feature samples to reduce redundancy and enhance generalization
hypotheses.result: POL improves cross-regional generalization on six architecture datasets

title: Using AI to forecast student dropout risk in technical education using a learning analytics approach
task: Predict student dropout risk in technical education
key_results: ML models identify at-risk students from 99,104 Moodle activity records (154 students); logistic regression and decision tree classifiers enable early intervention
comments: Weighted attribute method integrating engagement, difficulty, time allocation is sensible; limited quantitative performance metrics reported in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Weighted aggregation of behavioral and cognitive metrics predicts dropout risk better than individual signals
hypotheses.result: Enables early detection and adaptive pedagogical support

title: A pattern-growth approach for mining maximal fault-tolerant frequent itemsets
task: Mine maximal fault-tolerant frequent itemsets in noisy data
key_results: Pattern-growth FT-FP-tree framework substantially reduces execution time vs. existing Apriori-based algorithms; improved memory efficiency vs. other pattern-growth methods; single-pass tolerance evaluation
comments: First pattern-growth approach for FT-MFI mining; transaction mapping and conditional pattern extraction enhance scalability; addresses exponential candidate growth limitation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pattern-growth on FT-FP-tree with single-pass tolerance evaluation scales better than Apriori-based candidate generation
hypotheses.result: Substantial execution time reduction and improved memory efficiency on benchmark datasets

title: Analysis of anode temperature stability in hollow cathode vacuum arc brazing under multi-parameter coupling based on SR-MLP
task: Predict anode temperature stability in hollow cathode vacuum arc brazing
key_results: Combined SR-MLP model achieves MAE 0.83, RMSE 0.95, MAPE 3.49% on 10 validation welding parameter sets
comments: Physics-informed ML hybrid (COMSOL + neural network + symbolic regression) grounds predictions in multiphysics; limited to specific brazing geometry.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Anode temperature positively correlates with flow rate and current; negatively with spacing and tube radius
hypotheses.result: Verified by COMSOL simulations and validated on real welding parameter sets

title: Temporal dynamics in barrier-involved crashes: Determining shifts in driving behavior and injury risk across multi-year periods
task: Temporal dynamics in barrier-involved traffic crash injury severity
key_results: Random Parameter Logit and extensions reveal time-varying associations: moderate speed (35–55 mph) became protective in later periods; lane departure intensified as risk predictor; crashes outside intersections shifted from lower to elevated severity
comments: Sophisticated econometric modeling (MNL, RPL, RPLHM, CRPLHM, RPLHMV) captures unobserved heterogeneity and temporal instability; supports adaptive safety policies.

model_io.model: Multinomial Logit and Random Parameter Logit variants
inputs: barrier-crash data (roadway alignment, speed, weather, vehicle type, lane characteristics)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Predictor effects on crash severity shift across three multi-year periods (2017–18, 2019–20, 2021–22) due to behavioral and environmental changes
hypotheses.result: Speed zones, lane departure, median barriers, non-intersection location show temporal variation in effect

title: Enhancing educational assessment through automated question classification using a RoBERTa-based ensemble model
task: Automated exam question classification via Bloom's Taxonomy
key_results: RoBERTa + weighted ensemble (GRU/LSTM/CNN) achieves 92.37% accuracy, macro F1 0.923, AUC 0.992; McNemar's test confirms statistical significance over best single model (p=0.048)
comments: Ensemble approach well-motivated; transfer learning and weight combination leverage complementary classifier strengths; scalable for real-time assessment.

model_io.model: RoBERTa feature extraction + ensemble (GRU, LSTM, CNN)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Weighted ensemble of three diverse architectures outperforms individual models through complementary strengths
hypotheses.result: Ensemble 92.37% accuracy vs. best single model; improvement statistically significant (p=0.048)

title: Research on the application and evaluation of Mongolian saddle culture in kumis packaging design based on AIGC
task: Mongolian saddle cultural design for kumis packaging via AIGC
key_results: AHP-weighted design requirements + Fuzzy Comprehensive Evaluation validates AIGC-generated designs; demonstrates feasibility of 'human + AI' collaborative framework for cultural creative products
comments: Novel application of AIGC (text-to-image generation) for cultural resource transformation; AHP and FCE provide structure; lacks quantitative design performance metrics.

model_io.model: Analytic Hierarchy Process + Fuzzy Comprehensive Evaluation

hypotheses.hypothesis: AIGC enables effective transformation and expression of ethnic cultural elements in packaging design
hypotheses.result: Integration of AIGC significantly enhances efficiency and diversity of design generation

title: SIGMAformer: a spatiotemporal Gaussian mixture correlation transformer for global weather forecasting
task: Spatiotemporal weather forecasting via Gaussian mixture correlation
key_results: SIGMAformer outperforms state-of-the-art forecasting models; removal of DSTC increases MSE by 7.18% (wind speed) and 7.22% (temperature)
comments: Dynamic spatiotemporal correlation via Gaussian mixture pattern extraction elegantly weights critical patterns; scalable methodology for sensor-network fusion.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gaussian mixture pattern-specific weighting emphasizes critical spatiotemporal patterns while suppressing irrelevant ones
hypotheses.result: SIGMAformer outperforms baselines; DSTC removal increases MSE 7–7.2%, confirming importance

title: Intrinsically chiral exciton polaritons in an atomically-thin semiconductor
task: Induce strong coupling between chiral photons (BIC) and valley excitons in monolayer TMDCs
key_results: Valley-selective exciton polaritons with circularly polarised PL intensity and polarisation degree exceeding uncoupled excitons by order of magnitude; controllable upper/lower polariton spin alignment via excitation helicity
comments: The use of chiral BICs to create intrinsically chiral polaritons is novel; the folding mechanism suppressing depolarisation is physically insightful, though room-temperature coherence lifetime remains a practical limitation.

outputs: direct emission path shortcut to thermalisation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chiral BIC induces strong coupling with valley excitons preserving circular polarisation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; intrinsically chiral polaritons form with enhanced PL intensity and polarisation degree

hypotheses.hypothesis: Brillouin zone folding suppresses depolarisation via shortcut to thermalisation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; high-momenta polaritons access direct emission bypassing depolarising relaxation channels

title: Mid-infrared detection through ligand-driven local heating in lanthanide-doped nanoparticles
task: Enable mid-infrared detection via ligand-driven local heating in lanthanide nanoparticles
key_results: Detectivity of 4.8 × 10^8 Jones at 6.3 μm; broadband 5–10 μm detection with ~2 ms response time; competitive spectral fidelity and gas sensing vs. FTIR systems
comments: The ligand-mediated heating mechanism is elegant and represents a creative bridge between molecular and nanoscale design, though thermal drift and long-term stability require further investigation.

model_io.model: Ligand-capped lanthanide nanoparticles with mid-infrared absorption

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ligand organic molecules serve as efficient mid-infrared heat sources
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; broadband MIR absorption and local heating enable thermally modulated lanthanide emission

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thermally mediated detection achieves sensitivity competitive with FTIR
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; detectivity and spectral performance comparable to state-of-the-art FTIR systems

title: Impact of higher-order exchange on the lifetime of skyrmions and antiskyrmions
task: Calculate skyrmion and antiskyrmion lifetimes including higher-order exchange interactions
key_results: Higher-order exchange increases energy barriers and lifetime pre-factors by orders of magnitude; skyrmion stability achieved without DMI through four-spin tuning
comments: The atomistic spin model approach is rigorous, but experimental validation of the predicted lifetime enhancements in real materials remains pending.

model_io.model: Atomistic spin model with fourth-order exchange

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher-order exchange stabilizes skyrmions without DMI
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; skyrmions remain thermally stable when four-spin term is tuned appropriately

hypotheses.hypothesis: Four-spin interaction modulates lifetime pre-factor significantly
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pre-factor modulation spans orders of magnitude with small tuning

title: Visual perception of longitudinal waves: theory and observations
task: Characterize visual perception of longitudinal travelling wave motion
key_results: Longitudinal waves exhibit nonlinear perceptual splitting into forward crest and backward trough motion; speed for perceptual cancellation scales nonlinearly with oscillation amplitude
comments: This psychophysical study reveals fundamental nonlinearities in motion perception often overlooked by physics; the perceptual mechanism appears to decompose physical motion into emergent components.

model_io.model: Random-dot field simulation with sinusoidal oscillations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Longitudinal wave perception is uniform travelling wave motion
hypotheses.result: Rejected; perception splits into forward crest and backward trough components

hypotheses.hypothesis: Motion aftereffect occurs for all longitudinal wave amplitudes
hypotheses.result: Rejected; aftereffect only emerges when forward crest contrast is reduced

title: Engineering ligands for theophylline riboswitches expands its regulatory dynamic range in prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems
task: Enhance theophylline riboswitch dynamic range through ligand engineering
key_results: 4-quinazolinone derivatives achieve 30-fold higher binding affinity; bacterial ON expression enhanced 380-fold vs 75-fold with theophylline; 70% CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing efficiency at 10-fold lower ligand concentration
comments: Structure-based ligand design successfully translates across prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems, demonstrating practical value for biomedical applications.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Synthetic ligands with improved affinity will enhance riboswitch dynamic range across heterologous systems
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 380-fold ON activation in bacteria, 11-fold in eukaryotes with optimized ligands

hypotheses.hypothesis: Improved ligand binding reduces required ligand concentration for CRISPR control
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 70% efficiency at 10-fold lower concentration than theophylline

title: Single-cell spatial transcriptomic analysis of human skin anatomy
task: Map cellular composition, spatial organization, and immune-stromal interactions in human skin
key_results: Single-cell spatial atlas of 1.2 million cells from 114 samples; 45 cell types organized into 10 multicellular neighborhoods; TNF identified as central regulator of CCL19+ perivascular fibroblasts in immune-stromal crosstalk
comments: Integration of spatial transcriptomics with ligand-receptor analysis reveals disease-relevant architectural features, though mechanistic validation of identified interactions remains incomplete.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Skin cellular organization is structured into stereotypic multicellular neighborhoods with site-specific composition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 10 neighborhoods identified with consistent organization across anatomic sites

hypotheses.hypothesis: TNF signaling from perivascular cells maintains immune-stromal homeostasis
hypotheses.result: Supported by L-R analysis showing TNF-CCL19+ fibroblast interactions in perivascular neighborhood

hypotheses.hypothesis: Disease pathogenesis involves architectural disruption of perivascular neighborhoods
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pan-disease immune alterations localized to perivascular compartment
